children.text: CSS Images Module Level 3: image-orientation: none
The following images should not be identical.

rects.text: The following images should not be identical.

children.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is same origin.

rects.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is same origin.

children.text:
This image should rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none should be effectively ignored for opaque (cross-origin) images.

rects.text:
This image should rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none should be effectively ignored for opaque (cross-origin) images.

children.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is CORS anonymous.

rects.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is CORS anonymous.

children.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is CORS
       use-credentials.

rects.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the request is CORS
       use-credentials.

children.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the image source is a blob.

rects.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the image source is a blob.

children.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the image source is a data url.

rects.text:
The image should not rotate respecting their EXIF orientation because
       image-orientation: none is specified and the image source is a data url.
